channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Nadia Mukami Confesses Struggles in Relationship with Arrow Bwoy
item.description: Nadia Mukami, the Kenyan singer, shared her emotional journey with Arrow Bwoy during an interview, revealing her efforts to salvage their relationship. She admitted to simping, a term for pretending t...

item.title: Defense Secretary Teodoro Condemns China's Racist Propaganda Against Filipinos
item.description: Defense Secretary Gilbert Teodoro strongly criticized China Daily for its racist Facebook post depicting Filipinos as monkeys, calling it a revealing insight into the Chinese communist regime's disdai...

item.title: Impeachment Trial of Sara Duterte: Legal Battle Over Financial Records
item.description: The impeachment trial of Vice President Sara Duterte is facing legal challenges regarding the presentation of her tax and bank records. Prosecutors are seeking access to these documents, similar to th...

item.title: Harry Kane Reflects on England's Semi-Final Loss and Future World Cup Ambitions
item.description: England's captain, Harry Kane, expressed deep disappointment after his team's 2-1 semi-final defeat to Argentina in the 2026 FIFA World Cup. The Three Lions looked poised for their first World Cup fin...

item.title: Father seeks to attend son's wake after fatal stabbing in Caloocan
item.description: A father in poor health is requesting permission to attend the wake of his son, who was fatally stabbed in Caloocan. The incident has sparked community concern and raised questions about public safety...

item.title: Trinity Rodman Comments on NWSL Game Amid Poor Air Quality Concerns
item.description: The article discusses Trinity Rodman's comments on the National Women's Soccer League (NWSL) game between Gotham FC and the Washington Spirit, which drew a record crowd of 42,175 fans at Citi Field in...

item.title: Belgian Cyclist Tim Merlier Secures Third Stage Victory at Tour de France
item.description: Belgian sprinter Tim Merlier achieved his third stage win at the Tour de France, marking his sixth overall victory in the 113th edition of the race. The 12th stage concluded with a chaotic sprint fini...

item.title: US Congress Freezes Aid to Nigeria Amid Diplomatic Tensions
item.description: The article discusses the recent decision by US Republican lawmakers to restrict all non-humanitarian aid to Nigeria due to allegations of corruption, even as US Assistant Secretary Frank Garcia engag...

item.title: Calvin Tiggle Inducted into Canadian Football Hall of Fame for Outstanding Contributions
item.description: Calvin Tiggle, a veteran linebacker who played for the Hamilton Tiger-Cats and Toronto Argonauts, has been inducted into the Canadian Football Hall of Fame. The 57-year-old player, known for his defen...

item.title: Supreme Court reserves verdict on whether pending NAB bail applications should be heard by the SC or the Federal Constitutional Court
item.description: The Supreme Court of Pakistan has reserved its verdict on an important constitutional and legal question regarding whether it still has the jurisdiction to hear bail applications in pending National A...

item.title: Iran urges Houthis to block Red Sea route amid escalating US-Iran tensions
item.description: Iran has formally requested Yemen's Houthi movement to close the Red Sea oil shipping lane if the United States escalates military strikes against Iranian infrastructure. This demand comes amid renewe...

item.title: Arvind Kejriwal Backs Sonam Wangchuk's Hunger Strike, Demands Education Minister's Removal Over Exam Irregularities
item.description: Former Delhi chief minister and AAP national convener Arvind Kejriwal joined educator and climate activist Sonam Wangchuk at Jantar Mantar in New Delhi, extending support to Wangchuk's indefinite hung...

item.title: Nigerian Army Raids Lagos Hideout, Arrests 24 Foreign Nationals
item.description: The Nigerian Army’s 65 Battalion conducted a raid in Epe, Lagos State, arresting 24 foreign nationals from six West and Central African countries. The operation, part of the 81 Division's efforts to c...

item.title: South Korea Aims to Provide Universal AI Chatbot Access for All Residents
item.description: South Korea's government has initiated a tender to develop a universal basic AI chatbot accessible to all residents, aiming to ensure equitable access to AI services. The 'AI for everyone' plan involv...

item.title: Support Groups Offer Empathy for Hoarders Facing Eviction and Stigma
item.description: A peer support group in Wirral, Merseyside, is providing a safe space for individuals struggling with hoarding, a condition formally classified as a mental health issue by the World Health Organizatio...

item.title: Punjab CM Launches Education Reforms with 100,000 Free Bikes and Scholarships
item.description: Punjab Chief Minister Maryam Nawaz has announced a comprehensive education reform package aimed at modernizing schools and improving student safety. The initiative includes providing 100,000 free elec...

item.title: Man Finds New Love After Mother's Faith Guided Him Through Divorce
item.description: A man from Kiambu has shared an emotional story about how his mother's unwavering faith helped him heal after a painful divorce. The story, published on TUKO.co.ke, details how the separation devastat...

item.title: Fadiel Adams' complaints led to arrests of police officials in corruption probe
item.description: The Madlanga Commission revealed that National Coloured Congress MP Fadiel Adams initiated multiple criminal cases against former crime intelligence head Lt-Gen Dumisani Khumalo and other senior offic...

item.title: Strait of Hormuz conflict threatens global trade and security
item.description: The ongoing conflict over the Strait of Hormuz, a critical oil route, has shifted focus from Iran's nuclear program to control of the waterway. The US has reimposed naval blockades and considered toll...

item.title: First Nations Leader Calls for Etsy Fur Ban Reversal, Cites Discrimination Against Indigenous Artisans
item.description: Chief Jenny Brake of the Qalipu First Nation in Newfoundland and Labrador has criticized Etsy's decision to ban fur products, calling it discriminatory against Indigenous artisans and traditions. The ...
